title: Consolidate production on Vercel + AWS App Runner; retire Cloudflare/self-host as primary backend
statement: orcast production serving is consolidated on Vercel (frontend + edge proxy routes) and AWS App Runner (FastAPI backend: Bedrock, DynamoDB, S3, RDS, Step Functions). The Cloudflare-fronted self-hosted co-tenant (orcast-api.aimez.ai on the shared aimez-services EC2) is retired as the primary backend and kept dormant only as a one-env-var rollback. Both Vercel upstream env vars resolve to App Runner: ORCAST_API_BASE (generic /api/be JSON proxy) and ORCAST_STREAM_BASE (dedicated /api/narrate-stream SSE route).
rationale: Streaming requires a Cloudflare-free endpoint: WS-STREAM WS2 benchmarks proved the cloudflared quick tunnel buffers SSE completely across protocols and payloads, while App Runner streams incrementally. App Runner must run for streaming regardless, so co-hosting only the JSON path on the self-host adds fragility for little savings.

The self-host RDS path is unreachable (DD-6), which surfaced as a live console outage: orcast-api.aimez.ai returned 503 on POST /api/explore/sessions. App Runner reaches the explore RDS and returns 200, restoring the console.

The hackathon brief (Vercel x AWS workshop, H0_WORKSHOP_COMPLIANCE.md) requires a Vercel + AWS + Bedrock spine and does not require or mention Cloudflare. App Runner satisfies and exceeds that requirement.
Consolidation removes a transport hop (cloudflared) and a dependency (Cloudflare token/ingress) from the submission-critical path.

implementation.orcast_api_base: repointed to App Runner (Vercel env updated + redeployed)
implementation.iam: bedrock:InvokeModelWithResponseStream added to App Runner instance role (infra/aws/template.yaml + live)
implementation.self_host_disposition: left dormant as rollback; active teardown deferred to operator post-submission

non_claims: This does not physically tear down the self-host service or the Cloudflare ingress; both remain as a dormant rollback until an operator removes them.
This does not change the submitted live URL (orcast-h0.vercel.app); only the backend upstream resolves to App Runner.

rollback.method: set Vercel ORCAST_API_BASE back to https://orcast-api.aimez.ai and redeploy (requires the self-host RDS path to be repaired first)
